title: What I think about the fourth Amendment

transcript.text: Think about the Fourth Amendment, which is Search and Seizure. The right of the people
To be secure in their persons, houses, papers, and effects against unreasonable searches
And seizures shall not be violated and no warrants shall issue, but upon probable cause,
Stated by oath and affirmation, and particularly describing the place to be searched and the
Persons or things to be seized. So I guess back in the days of the English Empire, there
Was no respect for personal space. Like, you weren't, you didn't belong to yourself, you
Belonged to the king. And they could just come in your house and take your documents
In order to prove that your cousin is a witch and needs to be burned at the stake or something
Like this. Bad. Too bad they had to make that the Fourth Amendment. Thank God we have it.
Or maybe we would be searched. Because the thing is, if you search people, then that means
You can plant stuff in the house. You can just go and search anybody's house. And you could
Throw a bunch of child, naked child photos on the floor and say, oh, look what I found.
Ha, ha, ha, I'm in jail or whatever. So, yeah, thank God for the search warrants.
Um, now, but as far as the future of the Fourth Amendment, I think, uh, you know, as we go in
The future and get used to this whole Fourth Amendment, my stuff is my stuff, my rights to privacy is my
Right to privacy. When we get used to that, I think, uh, society, you know, will forget that it's okay,
Remember that it's okay, or, uh, remember that it's not okay to go and violate people's stuff.
And I think people will relax a little bit. And, um, and then, you know, not have stuff to hide
And be able to share stuff without freaking out. Um, you know, um, one thing I'm thinking
About here in terms of the future is, say, uh, family photos. A lot of people are afraid to upload
Their family photos on the internet and make them public because, I don't know, like, maybe
Superstitious people they'd be made fun of or people would laugh at them behind their back or
Something of this nature. But, I think the trend is going in the future that more and more people
Are going to become less free to share their family photos, opinions on issues, all the ways that
They express themselves, because more and more people are going to want, um, it's going to be
More and more socially acceptable and socially encouraged to share. And you see the beginnings of
This with people, uh, wanting, uh, attention, wanting fame. And, uh, you know, as more and more people
Open up, it'll motivate other people to open up and share. And what I see in the future is, um,
Everybody sharing everything. And not only sharing, but actually, you know, getting around eventually,
Maybe not this generation, but another generation, I don't know, actually getting in there and describing
Stuff. So, like, you know, if you want to, um, if your kid is doing a report on something, or, you know,
You want to make a montage or something, you can just go on Google and you can type, you know,
Four foot seven inch, half Mongolian, half African, blonde haired girl. And you'll actually get a photo
Of somebody like that. Because everybody shares, everybody describes, it's there. Um, and also in
Video. Like, if you go to the gym and you say, ooh, I want to see somebody, um, do his name and
Bench presses his name, you know, so I can motivate to try and do, like, I guess one more rip than I've
Ever done. So I could type in 14-year-old bench presses, 135 pounds 12 times. And then you get it.
And then you get to watch it and then go, okay, I can do 12 times. And, um, so that's what I see.
There's stuff just, uh, blowing open. And, um, I think, I don't really understand
Why people are afraid of their opinions and, um, you know, just use family photos as an example,
Because nobody has those up. I can guess that it's just, I guess, people are afraid
To go lose connections. And then, so they won't be able to get ahead in society, make fun of them.
And it's true, people do make fun of you if you share your opinions and your,
I'm going to direct it, let's get beyond, make fun of you, go and go, you know, like that, you know,
That's just words. Those are the Fourth Amendment, but I mean, I guess you have to have it.
If you don't, then, well, uh, there's a saying, absolute power corrupts absolutely.
If you get, if you let the cops, I don't want anybody to be going in your house and
Upturning everything, especially when there's laws that could make whatever they find
Illegal, like if you have marijuana under your bed, and that's illegal.
So, so maybe that's mainly what, uh, the Fourth Amendment is all about. It's protecting, um,
People from, uh, mainly being having stuff planted on their persons. I've had things planted on me.
I had a marijuana seed planted in my car once, and another occasion I had a joint planted in my
Friend's car, and he had to go to jail. I'd pay 600 bucks to go now. Um, so yeah, it definitely does happen.
So, it's just that it seems like there's lots of different facets for Fourth Amendment. One of them is,
You know, so that people don't plant stuff on you. Another one is so that people can't
Use information about you that's true to, uh, bust you, which is definitely isn't fair.
And then there's the other thing about, um, the right to privacy, which is kind of, I think,
In the superstitious realm. But, you know, I think that's common knowledge that we respect that.
And maybe, uh, we didn't before. So that's about what I think about the Fourth Amendment without
Doing any reading on it. It's just off the top of my head. But it's the only way I'm going to upload a
Video on it. I'm not going to read about the Fourth Amendment any time soon. So, think about it.
